objective: Repair the PR quality gate after a malformed workflow edit introduced duplicated YAML.
summary: The PR quality workflow was rewritten cleanly and re-read to confirm the specific active-spec gate and no-spec exemption logic remained intact.
assumptions: The malformed workflow edit was isolated to the PR quality gate and did not affect unrelated CI jobs.

evidence.summary: The first workflow patch showed duplicated YAML content and had to be replaced with a clean full-file rewrite.
The rewritten workflow and PR template were read back after editing to confirm the spec gate matched the template.

taskLedger.notes: Breadth scan isolated the duplicated YAML and kept only the workflow plus template context live.
Raw diff context was compressed into the episode summary after the workflow rewrite completed.

verification.details: Both files were read back after the rewrite and the spec-reference rule matched the PR template guidance.
The workflow was not executed from this interface, so YAML and runtime validation still require a local or CI run.

residualRisks: Workflow syntax still depends on a real local or CI execution pass for full validation.
